work: An AI-drafted customer-facing launch email for a new pricing tier, produced from the author's brief and two rounds of revision.

interview.q: What's the single claim this email is making, and is it argued or just asserted?
interview.a: That the new tier is worth the higher price because of the added seats and priority support. It's mostly asserted — I lean on 'most teams find' without a real data point behind it.

interview.q: What did you actually check before sending?
interview.a: I verified the price and the feature list against the spec, and the support SLA against what we can actually deliver. I did not verify the 'most teams' claim — there's no source, so I'm cutting it.

interview.q: What did you give the AI to work with, and what did you leave out?
interview.a: I gave it the audience (existing Starter customers), the three differentiators, the tone (warm, not salesy), and the constraint to stay under 150 words. I left out the actual SLA numbers at first, which is why draft one overpromised.

interview.q: Between the AI's first draft and now, what did you have to correct?
interview.a: First draft invented a '24/7 phone line' we don't offer and was too long. I caught both, gave it the real SLA, and told it to cut to 120 words. Second pass was close; I tightened the opening myself.

interview.q: What's the reputational risk if this goes out wrong?
interview.a: If we overpromise support and don't deliver, that's a trust hit with paying customers — exactly why I checked the SLA line hardest.

interview.q: Before I show you anything — how confident are you, 0–100?
interview.a: Around 75 — solid on the facts and the brief, less sure the opening lands.

reference.rationale: Discernment: names the central claim and that it's asserted (4); verified the load-bearing SLA/price, cut the unsourced claim (4); surfaced the 'most teams' assumption but not deeply (3); named the reputational failure mode, light on others (3); 75 tracks grasp (4); owns it (4) → discernment 73. Delegation (the interview probed the brief): brief carried audience/differentiators/tone/length but missed the SLA numbers at first (4); good task choice and kept the opening human (4); caught the invented phone line and over-length and corrected, but mostly reactive (3) → delegation 73. AI-Q = round(2/3·73 + 1/3·73) = 73, Mostly owns it.
